content.text: Achieving an initial SOC 2 report or ISO 27001/42001 certification is only the start. Maintaining the certification requires a disciplined annual cadence of surveillance audits, internal assessments, management reviews, and continuous evidence collection. For GenAI systems, this maintenance cycle must also include AI-specific activities — bias evaluations, red-team exercises, and model-change reviews — that have no equivalent in traditional IT certification programmes.

: a certification that lapses or is suspended is typically more damaging to enterprise customer relationships than never having been certified. Build the maintenance cadence into the team's operating rhythm before the initial certificate is issued.
Annual report covers a defined period (6–12 months); new report required each year
New fieldwork each year (no surveillance concept — each engagement is fresh)
Annual — each Type II report is effectively a recertification
Certificate valid 3 years from initial certification
Two surveillance audits per year (at ~6 and ~12 months after cert date)

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import date, timedelta
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional

class CertificationType(str, Enum):
    SOC2_TYPE2   = "soc2_type2"
    ISO_27001    = "iso_27001"
    ISO_42001    = "iso_42001"
    ISO_42001_ISMS_COMBINED = "iso_42001_isms_combined"

class AuditEventKind(str, Enum):
    INITIAL_CERTIFICATION   = "initial_certification"
    SURVEILLANCE            = "surveillance"
    RECERTIFICATION         = "recertification"
    INTERNAL_AUDIT          = "internal_audit"
    MANAGEMENT_REVIEW       = "management_review"
    MOCK_AUDIT              = "mock_audit"
    BIAS_EVALUATION         = "bias_evaluation"
    RED_TEAM                = "red_team"
    CONTROL_TEST_CYCLE      = "control_test_cycle"

@dataclass
class AuditEvent:
    event_id: str
    kind: AuditEventKind
    certification: Optional[CertificationType]
    target_date: date
    responsible: str         # team / individual
    status: str = "planned"  # "planned" | "in_progress" | "complete" | "overdue"
    completed_at: Optional[date] = None
    notes: str = ""

    def is_overdue(self) -> bool:
        return (self.status not in ("complete",) and
                date.today() > self.target_date)

@dataclass
class CertificationRecord:
    cert_type: CertificationType
    first_certified: date
    current_cert_date: date
    current_cert_expiry: date
    certification_body: str
    audit_period_months: int     # SOC 2: 6 or 12; ISO: 12 between surveillances
    recertification_years: int   # ISO: 3; SOC 2: annual (each report is a new Type II)
    next_surveillance: Optional[date] = None
    scope_statement: str = ""
    last_scope_change: Optional[date] = None

    def days_until_expiry(self) -> int:
        return (self.current_cert_expiry - date.today()).days

    def recertification_due(self) -> date:
        years = self.recertification_years
        return date(self.current_cert_date.year + years,
                    self.current_cert_date.month,
                    self.current_cert_date.day)

class CertificationCalendar:
    """
    Generates the full maintenance event schedule for all active certifications.
    Accounts for SOC 2 annual report cycle and ISO 3-year recertification.
    """
    SOC2_FIELDWORK_LEAD_WEEKS = 8    # fieldwork starts 8 weeks before report date
    ISO_SURV_COUNT_PER_YEAR = 2      # two surveillance audits per year before recert
    MOCK_AUDIT_LEAD_DAYS = 60        # mock audit runs 60 days before real fieldwork

    def __init__(self, certs: list[CertificationRecord]):
        self._certs = certs

    def upcoming_events(self, horizon_days: int = 365) -> list[AuditEvent]:
        """Returns all events due within the horizon, sorted by target date."""
        cutoff = date.today() + timedelta(days=horizon_days)
        events = []
        for cert in self._certs:
            events.extend(self._events_for_cert(cert, cutoff))
        return sorted(events, key=lambda e: e.target_date)

    def _events_for_cert(
        self, cert: CertificationRecord, cutoff: date
    ) -> list[AuditEvent]:
        events = []
        today = date.today()

        if cert.cert_type == CertificationType.SOC2_TYPE2:
            # SOC 2: annual fieldwork + report cycle
            report_date = date(today.year, cert.current_cert_expiry.month,
                               cert.current_cert_expiry.day)
            if report_date < today:
                report_date = date(today.year + 1, report_date.month, report_date.day)
            fieldwork_start = report_date - timedelta(weeks=self.SOC2_FIELDWORK_LEAD_WEEKS)
            mock_date = fieldwork_start - timedelta(days=self.MOCK_AUDIT_LEAD_DAYS)
            for evt_date, kind, responsible in [
                (mock_date,       AuditEventKind.MOCK_AUDIT,    "risk-team"),
                (fieldwork_start, AuditEventKind.SURVEILLANCE,  "cpa-firm"),
                (report_date,     AuditEventKind.RECERTIFICATION, "cpa-firm"),
            ]:
                if today <= evt_date <= cutoff:
                    events.append(AuditEvent(
                        event_id=f"{cert.cert_type.value}-{evt_date}",
                        kind=kind,
                        certification=cert.cert_type,
                        target_date=evt_date,
                        responsible=responsible,
                    ))

        elif cert.cert_type in (CertificationType.ISO_27001, CertificationType.ISO_42001,
                                CertificationType.ISO_42001_ISMS_COMBINED):
            # ISO: two surveillance audits per year; recertification every 3 years
            for offset_months in [6, 12]:
                surv_date = date(
                    cert.current_cert_date.year + (offset_months // 12),
                    cert.current_cert_date.month if offset_months < 12
                    else cert.current_cert_date.month,
                    cert.current_cert_date.day,
                )
                if today <= surv_date <= cutoff:
                    events.append(AuditEvent(
                        event_id=f"{cert.cert_type.value}-surv-{surv_date}",
                        kind=AuditEventKind.SURVEILLANCE,
                        certification=cert.cert_type,
                        target_date=surv_date,
                        responsible="certification-body",
                    ))
            recert_date = cert.recertification_due()
            if today <= recert_date <= cutoff:
                events.append(AuditEvent(
                    event_id=f"{cert.cert_type.value}-recert-{recert_date}",
                    kind=AuditEventKind.RECERTIFICATION,
                    certification=cert.cert_type,
                    target_date=recert_date,
                    responsible="certification-body",
                ))
        return events
Adding new AI systems to the certification scope between audit cycles is a common challenge in fast-moving GenAI programmes. Track scope changes and assess whether they are material:

from datetime import datetime

@dataclass
class ScopeChangeEvent:
    """
    Tracks changes to the certification scope (new AI systems, new services,
    dropped services). Scope changes may require a scope extension assessment
    by the certification body between surveillance audits.
    """
    change_id: str
    cert_type: CertificationType
    change_date: date
    description: str          # what changed
    new_systems_added: list[str]    # AI system IDs added to scope
    systems_removed: list[str]
    requires_cb_notification: bool  # must the certification body be notified?
    cb_notified_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    extension_assessment_required: bool = False
    extension_assessment_date: Optional[date] = None

def assess_scope_change(
    change: ScopeChangeEvent,
    materiality_threshold_pct: float = 0.20,
    current_system_count: int = 10,
) -> dict:
    """
    A scope change is material (requires CB notification + possible extension)
    if the new systems represent > materiality_threshold_pct of the in-scope population.
    """
    added_pct = len(change.new_systems_added) / max(current_system_count, 1)
    is_material = (added_pct > materiality_threshold_pct or
                   change.requires_cb_notification)
    return {
        "material": is_material,
        "added_pct": round(added_pct * 100, 1),
        "action": (
            "Notify CB; schedule extension assessment"
            if is_material and change.extension_assessment_required
            else "Notify CB; record in next surveillance"
            if is_material
            else "Log internally; disclose in next surveillance"
        ),
    }
SOC 2 does not have a 'surveillance' model — each engagement covers a new audit period:
ISO Surveillance Audit — What the Auditor Tests
ISO surveillance audits are shorter than initial certification audits (typically 50–70% of the initial audit days). The auditor focuses on:

content: : every finding from the previous audit or surveillance must have a closed corrective action with verified evidence.
: new AI systems, scope changes, significant control changes, incidents, and management review outputs.
: the auditor samples controls that were effective at the prior audit to confirm they are still operating.
: the standard requires at least one internal audit per year. The auditor will review the internal audit report and its findings.
: minutes from the management review must be available with evidence that all required inputs were addressed.

content.text: GenAI systems require maintenance activities that have no direct parallel in a traditional ISO 27001 or SOC 2 programme. These must be scheduled and evidenced as part of the certification maintenance cycle:
Whether model outputs have changed demographic parity, equal opportunity, or calibration since last eval
Bias evaluation report with metric deltas; sign-off by AI ethics officer
Adversarial prompts, jailbreak attempts, indirect injection, model extraction — against current production model
Red-team report; findings and mitigations; executive summary
On each model version change; minimum annual
Whether the model card accurately describes the current production model's capabilities, limitations, and risk profile
Updated model card with version and review date
Whether LLM vendor's SOC 2 / ISO 27001 is still current and unqualified
Cert tracker export; updated TPRM score
Whether risk ratings for each AI use case are still accurate given model changes, new regulations, incidents
Updated risk assessment records with approval
Enterprise AI programmes deploy new use cases continuously. Every new use case that uses the certified infrastructure must be evaluated for scope inclusion:

# scope_change_assessment.yaml — completed when a new AI use case is deployed
scope_change_id: SC-2024-007
cert_types_affected: [soc2_type2, iso_27001, iso_42001]
change_date: "2024-10-15"
description: >
  New AI use case: contract review assistant deployed to Legal team.
  Uses the existing inference gateway (in scope). Processes confidential
  legal documents (new data category).

new_systems_added: [use-case-contract-review-v1]
systems_removed: []

materiality_assessment:
  new_data_category: true        # confidential legal documents — heightened risk
  new_system_count: 1
  current_scope_count: 12
  added_pct: 8.3%
  material: true                 # new data category triggers material classification

required_actions:
  - action: Notify certification body (ISO 27001 + 42001)
    due: "2024-10-22"
    owner: risk-steward

  - action: Conduct AI impact assessment for contract review use case
    due: "2024-11-15"
    owner: data-protection-officer

  - action: Add use case to SOC 2 scope statement for next audit period
    due: next_audit_period_start
    owner: risk-steward

  - action: Update SOC 2 description of services document
    due: next_audit_period_start
    owner: risk-steward
The highest-risk event in a certification programme is an unintended lapse. Common causes and mitigations:
Fieldwork start missed due to resource conflict
No fieldwork kickoff meeting 12 weeks before period end
Calendar the fieldwork start date the day the current report is issued; block resource calendars
Open action at T-30 days before surveillance
Weekly action-tracker review from T-60 days; escalate to CISO if open at T-21 days
No audit scheduled by Q3 of the certification year
Book internal audit in Q1; treat as a fixed commitment not moveable without CISO approval
Schedule management review in Q1 as a standing annual governance meeting
New AI use case deployed without scope assessment
Gate new AI deployments: require scope assessment sign-off before go-live

Certification maintenance is a continuous programme, not a point-in-time project. For SOC 2, the annual cycle starts the day the current report is issued. For ISO 27001 and 42001, the three-year recertification cycle has two surveillance audits per year, each requiring completed internal audits, closed corrective actions, and a management review. GenAI programmes add AI-specific maintenance obligations — quarterly bias evaluations, annual red-team exercises, and model card currency reviews — that must be evidenced at each surveillance. Build these activities into the team's annual operating calendar before the initial certificate is issued, and treat scope changes as a formal governance event requiring a materiality assessment and, where needed, certification body notification.
